note: LLM in the loop: Claude (claude-opus-4-8) ranked the vetted rescue paths and the executor sent its choice to a live EVM. The deterministic core sized every path and enforced the spend cap + action whitelist BEFORE ranking, so the model's only freedom was which safe path to take.
scenario: mEURC collateral, USDC debt; EURC drifts down until HF crosses the Mandate trigger

viablePaths.why: TOP-UP 0.94 USDC from reserve (no swap, no slippage, no collateral sold)
ROTATE 7.00 USDC of mEURC(LT 0.800) → mTBILL(LT 0.900) — PARTIAL: only reaches HF 1.260 (target 1.380, capped)
DELEVERAGE: sell 2.24 USDC of mEURC, repay debt — PARTIAL: only reaches HF 0.820 (target 1.380)

ranking.memo: HF 1.120 < trigger 1.200. Target 1.380. Debt 5.00 USDC. Candidates: TOP-UP, ROTATE, DELEVERAGE. LLM ranked 3 viable paths and chose TOP-UP 0.94 USDC from reserve (no swap, no slippage, no collateral sold) (cost 0.00 USDC). Reason: The top-up fully restores your health factor to the 1.380 target using your prepaid reserve — no swap, no slippage, no collateral sold, and zero cost now. The other options only partially fix the position and leave it still below target.
